summary: Jeff Atwood announces the completion of his .NET API for controlling BetaBrite LED signs, which he previously mentioned developing for automated continuous integration feedback. The API wraps the Alpha Sign Communications Protocol and communicates via RS-232 serial connection. He has published the code as an article on CodeProject and provides a brief VB.NET code sample demonstrating how to allocate memory, set text with transitions, and control the sign's display sequence.
key_points: The API provides a simple .NET wrapper around the Alpha Sign Communications Protocol for BetaBrite LED signs
Physical hardware requirements include a serial port or USB-to-Serial adapter with a 25-50 foot RS-232 to RJ-12 cable
The code demonstrates memory allocation, text setting with various transitions (Rotate, WipeLeft, RollDown), and sequencing capabilities
money_quotes: Now go get you some of that sweet, hot LED sign action!
key_insight: Physical computing projects like LED sign integration can be made accessible through clean API abstractions that hide the complexity of serial communication protocols.
